stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 20 website users. The sample mean is 279.6 ms with a sample standard deviation of 104.2 ms. However, 3 of the 20 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 17 observations have a mean of 270.0 ms and standard deviation of 79.3 ms. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 279.6, 95% CI = [230.8, 328.4]. Without outliers: mean = 270.0, 95% CI = [231.5, 308.5]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [230.8, 328.4]. Point estimate: 274.8 ms.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [230.8, 328.4].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
